original_hypothesis.title: Artist Can Toggle Soulbound Status After Minting, Retroactively Locking or Unlocking Already-Distributed Tokens
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: PhiNFT1155.safeTransferFrom checks soulBounded(id_) which dynamically queries PhiFactory.artData(artId).soulBounded. The artist can call updateArtSettings to change soulBounded at any time, even after tokens are minted. Setting soulBounded=true retroactively makes all existing tokens non-transferable. Setting soulBounded=false on a previously soulbound token enables transfers of credential tokens that were supposed to be non-transferable.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Soulbound status is a critical property that affects token economics and credential integrity. Dynamic toggling undermines user expectations and marketplace listings.

proof_of_signal.pattern: safeTransferFrom checks soulBounded(id_) which calls phiFactoryContract.artData(_tokenIdToArtId[tokenId_]).soulBounded — dynamically read from mutable storage

preconditions: Artist (art creator) decides to change soulbound status
Tokens have already been minted to holders

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Soulbound status dynamically queried from mutable factory storage, not snapshot at mint

operational_questions: Can a malicious artist list tokens as transferable, wait for secondary sales, then toggle to soulbound?
Does toggling soulbound=false on credential tokens undermine protocol integrity?
Are there marketplace implications for retroactive soulbound changes?
validation_requirements: Create art with soulBounded=false, mint tokens, toggle to true
Test reverse: soulBounded=true -> false -> successful transfer

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Users mint and list on secondary markets
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: All listed tokens become non-transferable, trapping marketplace listings

potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Reversible by toggling back, but damage may be done
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: All transfers and marketplace activity for affected tokens

execution_requirements_analysis.coordination_complexity: Low — single transaction by art creator

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: updateArtSettings calls changing soulBounded after numberMinted > 0
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Transfer failures for previously transferable tokens
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Soulbound toggle correlated with marketplace activity

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
